_ai.instruction: This file defines the CLI behavior for generating React projects using templates and presets. AI agents can use this to guide interactive or programmatic project creation.
_ai.expectedUse: Provides schema and data for decision making during app generation.

metadata.name: pwa-kit-create-app
metadata.description: This file contains the schema for the pwa-kit-create-app CLI tool. Includes the schemas for presets, templates, validators, and questions. It also includes the data for those presets, templates, and validators.

_ai.instruction: Use this during project creation when piping in answers from stdin. It's very important that you include the template id keyed as `general.presetOrTemplateId` in the answers object. This can be derived from the `templateId` property of the preset object or the `id` property of the template object.
_ai.expectedUse: This data object is used to store the answers to the template questions asked by the program and answered by the user.

_ai.instruction: This value maps directly to the key in the answers object.
_ai.expectedUse: Use this to store or retrieve the corresponding answer.

_ai.instruction: This defines the prompt type (e.g., list, input, confirm). Determines how to display the question.
_ai.expectedUse: Configure the appropriate UI for asking the question.

_ai.instruction: Only used when type is 'list', 'rawlist', 'checkbox', or similar. These are the selectable options.
_ai.expectedUse: Render multiple choice options to the user.

_ai.instruction: Defines the questions to be asked to the user or AI agent to gather required input for project generation.
_ai.expectedUse: Used to drive interactive or programmatic question-and-answer flows.

_ai.instruction: These are default answers pre-filled when this preset is selected. They should be merged with user-supplied answers, where user-supplied values take precedence.
_ai.expectedUse: Auto-filling answers to questions without prompting the user unless a value is missing or overridden.

_ai.instruction: This property is used to filter what presets are shown to the user. If the preset is private, it should NOT be shown to the user in a list of selectable presets.
_ai.expectedUse: Hiding presets from the user.

_ai.instruction: This property is used as a source of all available presets. When an AI agent is asked to select a preset, the selected preset should be returned as the 'general.presetOrTemplateId' property including all the presets answers under the 'answers' property.
_ai.expectedUse: List of all available presets.

_ai.instruction: When creating a new project using a template, this property should be renamed to `presetOrTemplateId` and is used to identify the template.
_ai.expectedUse: Used as a unique identifier for the template.

_ai.instruction: This defines how the template is sourced. If 'npm', then 'name' must be the NPM package name. If 'bundle', the template is included directly in the CLI.
_ai.expectedUse: Used to determine how to fetch and prepare the template for project creation.

_ai.instruction: This property references the Inquirer Question interface defined here https://github.com/DefinitelyTyped/DefinitelyTyped/blob/master/types/inquirer/index.d.ts
_ai.expectedUse: Instruct agent on how to ask the user questions.

_ai.instruction: These are default answers pre-filled when this template is selected. They should be merged with user-supplied answers, where user-supplied values take precedence.
_ai.expectedUse: Auto-filling answers to questions without prompting the user unless a value is missing or overridden.

_ai.instruction: This property is used to filter what templates are shown to the user.
_ai.expectedUse: Hiding templates from the user.

_ai.instruction: Do not display templates that are private. Answer is a key/value pair where the key is `project.template` and the value is the template id.
_ai.expectedUse: Asking the user which template they want to use.

id.description: The unique identifier for the validator.

description.description: A brief description of what the validator does.

message.description: The error message to display if validation fails.

_ai.instruction: When validation fails, use this message to inform the user what went wrong.
_ai.expectedUse: For validation error feedback.

regex.description: The validation logic, often a regular expression or a reference to a function.

_ai.instruction: Use this regex to validate the user input.
_ai.expectedUse: For validation of user input.

_ai.instruction: These validators can be applied to template questions to ensure valid input. Each validator has a unique `id` that is referenced from questions.
_ai.expectedUse: Validating user input for questions during project creation.

_ai.instruction: This option modifies the behavior of the CLI tool. Use `defaultValue` if the option is not provided.
_ai.expectedUse: Determine which CLI flags can be included when generating the command.

_ai.instruction: These are the options/arguments that can be passed to the @salesforce/pwa-kit-create-app CLI tool. Do not use options that aren't listed here.
_ai.expectedUse: Instruct agent on how to use the @salesforce/pwa-kit-create-app CLI tool to create a new PWA Kit project.

_ai.instruction: Each example shows how to use the CLI tool with real command-line input.
_ai.expectedUse: Show examples in documentation or when the agent is generating usage examples.

presets.name: Retail React App Demo
presets.description: Generate a project using the settings for a special B2C Commerce instance that is used for demo purposes. No questions are asked. Use this preset to try out PWA Kit.
presets.shortDescription: The Retail app with demo Commerce Cloud instance

presets.name: Retail React App Demo Store
presets.description: Generates a project using the settings for a special B2C Commerce instance that is used for demo purposes. The demo site is accessible at https://pwa-kit.mobify-storefront.com/ This environment uses a SLAS private client and has social and passwordless login enabled. This environment is set up to use multiple locales. Future features that are enabled for the demo environment may be added to this preset.
presets.shortDescription: The Retail app with demo Commerce Cloud instance and a private SLAS client

presets.name: Retail React App Private SLAS client project
Retail React App Bug Bounty Project
Retail React App Hybrid Test Private SLAS Project
Retail React App Hybrid Test Public SLAS client project

templates.name: Retail React App
templates.description: Generate a project using custom settings by answering questions about a B2C Commerce instance. Use this preset to connect to an existing instance, such as a sandbox.
templates.shortDescription: The Retail app using your own Commerce Cloud instance

questions.message: Do you wish to use template extensibility?

templates.description: Generate a project using a bare-bones TypeScript app template. Use this as a TypeScript starting point or as a base on top of which to build new TypeScript project templates for Managed Runtime.

questions.message: What is the name of your Project?

validators.name: Valid Project Name
validators.description: Ensures the project name is valid (letters, numbers, space, hyphens, 1-20 characters).
validators.message: Value can only contain letters, numbers, space and hyphens; with a maximum length is 20 characters.

validators.name: Valid URL
validators.description: Ensures the value is an absolute URL.
validators.message: Value must be an absolute URL

validators.name: Valid Site ID
validators.description: Ensures the Site ID is valid.
validators.message: Valid characters are alphanumeric, hyphen, or underscore

validators.name: Valid Short Code
validators.description: Ensures the short code is valid.
validators.message: Invalid format. Use docs to find more information about valid configurations: https://developer.salesforce.com/docs/commerce/commerce-api/guide/commerce-api-configuration-values

validators.name: Valid Client ID
validators.description: Ensures the client ID is valid.
validators.message: Invalid format. Use docs to find more information about valid configurations: https://developer.salesforce.com/docs/commerce/commerce-api/guide/base-url.html

validators.name: Valid Organization ID
validators.description: Ensures the organization ID is valid.
validators.message: Invalid format. Use docs to find more information about valid configurations: https://developer.salesforce.com/docs/commerce/commerce-api/guide/base-url.html

options.name: --outputDir <path>
options.description: Path to the output directory for the new project

options.name: --preset <name>
options.description: The name of a project preset to use.

options.name: --templateVersion <version>
options.description: The version of the template to be generated when it's source is NPM.

options.name: --verbose
options.description: Print additional logging information to the console.

options.name: --stdio
options.description: Accept project generation answers from stdin as JSON

options.name: --displayProgram
options.description: Display the program.json file detailing the program schema/data and exit
